paragraphs.text: Jacob, having blessed his sons, died at the age of one hundred and forty-seven years. Joseph came with the elders of Egypt and with all the household of his father, and they buried Jacob with his fathers. Then Joseph and all those with him returned to Egypt. The brothers feared Joseph, and said: “Thy father charged us before his death to say unto thee: Forgive… the trespass and the sin of thy brethren, in that they did evil unto thee… And Joseph wept… Fear not me (Gen 50:16–17, 19). Though your father is dead, the God of your father is alive. For His sake I will do you no harm, because the evil you devised God turned to good for me and put into my hand a numerous people. Therefore I will do no wrong to those who caused that the life of many was saved. But as I have not failed to provide for you in Egypt, so do you also not leave my bones in Egypt.” And he made them swear, saying: “God will surely visit you, and shall bring you up out of this land… unto the land which He swore… to Abraham… ye shall carry up my bones with you. If I shall not inherit together with you the promised inheritance, then together with you shall I rise again. So Joseph died, being a hundred and ten years old… and they put him in a coffin in Egypt” (Gen 50:24–26).
